tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(4,12): error TS1005: ';' expected.
tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(4,13): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'ping'.
tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(7,12): error TS1005: ';' expected.
tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(11,12): error TS1005: ';' expected.
tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(11,13): error TS1128: Declaration or statement expected.
tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(11,17): error TS1109: Expression expected.
tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(16,13): error TS1477: An instantiation expression cannot be followed by a property access.
tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(16,22): error TS2339: Property 'ping' does not exist on type '(self: {}, x: number) => number'.
tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua(20,24): error TS1005: '=>' expected.


==== tluaColonCallParseErrors.tlua (9 errors) ====
    local M = { ping = function(self: {}) return 1; end };
    
    // A colon access exists only as a call: Lua rejects `obj:f` without arguments.
    local f = M:ping;
               ~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
                ~~~~
!!! error TS2304: Cannot find name 'ping'.
    
    // The colon needs a member name after it.
    local g = M:(1);
               ~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
    
    // Lua reserved words cannot name a colon call: committing on `M:end(` would
    // swallow the enclosing block terminator.
    local h = M:end();
               ~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
                ~~~
!!! error TS1128: Declaration or statement expected.
                    ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    
    // An instantiation expression cannot be followed by a colon call, matching the
    // dot form's diagnostic.
    declare id: <T>(self: {}, x: T) => T;
    local i = id<number>:ping();
                ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS1477: An instantiation expression cannot be followed by a property access.
                         ~~~~
!!! error TS2339: Property 'ping' does not exist on type '(self: {}, x: number) => number'.
    
    // Error recovery: an annotation-shaped `ident : ident` in invalid code must
    // not swallow the declarations that follow it into a bogus argument list.
    local bad = (b: number);
                           ~
!!! error TS1005: '=>' expected.
    declare fine: (n: number) => void;
    fine(1);
